parsed_review.summary: CP00-381 (Risk B, 40 units, RP12.P06.M07.S07-RP12.P06.M09.S02, billing core p06 test/Hermes slice descriptor) passes independent read-only review. The pack-level production_ready claim is NOT prematurely asserted: command-evidence records production_ready=false with status "implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout", and the Hermes packet's production_ready_candidate=true is correctly a candidate pending human final approval. Identity and scope are consistent across pack binding (registry.js:290), requirements (registry.js:1649), contract current_pack + p06_test_hermes_slice_descriptor projection (billing-core-contract.json:16,20699), the closeout-pack-plan entry (closeout-pack-plan.json:412), and the review baseline: Risk B, 40 units, upstream CP00-380, next CP00-382, next_subphase RP12.P06.M09.S03; the live cursor (next_subphase RP12.P06.M07.S07) confirms CP00-381 is the legitimate next pack. Deliverable distribution (implementation 12 / security_audit 10 / ui 8 / claude_review 2 / test 8 = 40) and micro-title rows (16/22/2) were independently counted from included_units and match the enforced requirements and validateBillingCoreCp381Coverage. All 7 required public exports exist (service.js:2230,2263; validators.js:3336,3371,3427,3448,3471), are surfaced via the index.js wildcard re-export, and are exercised by tests (model.test.js:1192-1242). Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: freezeCp381Result (service.js:2182) sets all runtime/write/leak flags false and no_real_data true, no fs/network/db/exec side-effects exist in the billing source, and the no-write attestation spread chain CP364 to CP381 is unbroken (promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false). Authority boundary is correct: Claude is a read-only reviewer with tools Read/Grep/Glob and is not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, read_only=true); human approval gates runtime opening, deferred to CP00-382. Hermes control-plane usage is limited to a descriptor-only, no-write evidence packet/receipt, not product runtime (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false). Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied: readiness auth_preflight passed with valid (non-empty, well-formed) JSON output, the prompt embeds no full diff/source, summary-only review is correctly prohibited for this Risk B pack, no forbidden tools are present, and all required validation commands are recorded at exit_code 0. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one cosmetic scope-report labeling note (P3) that does not affect closeout.

findings.title: Pack closeout dir listed under both untracked implementation files and unrelated-dirty-preserved in changed-file-scope
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-381/ appears simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (line 132) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 138); the same dual-listing applies to docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md. The 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved' label implies the entry is outside pack scope, yet the directory holds this pack's own manifest.json and command-evidence.json closeout evidence. This is a categorization overlap in the scope-report generator, not a defect in the pack itself. The material safety invariant active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no required implementation file was dropped from review, and both the manifest and command-evidence were available and inspected.
findings.recommendation: In the scope-report generator, make untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved mutually exclusive (e.g., exclude entries already classified as implementation-related from the unrelated-preserved list) so the report is unambiguous. No change required to the CP00-381 deliverables.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic bookkeeping note. Does not affect production_ready determination, scope coverage, or closeout; nothing material omitted (active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty).
